data.title: Review resolution loop gives up at score=3 instead of addressing valid important findings
data.description:
## Summary
During sprint batch 5 review, dso:code-reviewer-standard repeatedly scored correctness=3 with valid important findings (e.g., slugManuallyEdited flag bug). The resolution loop ran 4+ passes across standard and deep tiers but the agent suggested graceful shutdown instead of applying the fixes.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run /dso:sprint with a batch that triggers standard review
2. Review finds valid important findings scoring correctness=3
3. Resolution sub-agent applies some fixes but not all
4. Re-review still scores 3 on remaining valid findings
5. Agent suggests graceful shutdown instead of continuing to address findings

## Expected Behavior
The resolution loop should continue applying valid fixes for important findings that score <= 3 until either: (a) all important findings are resolved, or (b) the max resolution attempts are exhausted with a clear escalation message.

## Actual Behavior
The agent gives up after 4+ passes and suggests graceful shutdown, even though the remaining findings (middleware cookie test, page.tsx routing test, Layout.tsx hardcoded empty projects, slugManuallyEdited flag reset) are addressable code changes.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
The resolution sub-agent may be treating score=3 as 'close enough' rather than recognizing that important findings with concrete fix suggestions should be addressed. The review-fix-dispatch.md prompt may not emphasize that the agent should implement ALL suggested fixes, not just the easiest ones.

## Log Context
Agent: dso:code-reviewer-standard (Standard review batch 5 final)
Scores: hygiene=5, design=5, maintainability=4, correctness=3, verification=4
Finding 1: slugManuallyEdited flag resetting to false (valid, easy fix)
